title: zehneria somalensis
intro: Zehneria somalensis, also known as the Somali snake root, is a plant species within the Cucurbitaceae family. It is traditionally used in various regions for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating snake bites and other venomous stings. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antivenom properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with snake bites, such as pain, swelling, and inflammation. In some regions, Zehneria somalensis is prepared as a paste or infusion, typically made by crushing the plant's roots and mixing it with water or other substances, to be used as a treatment for various health issues.
